howItWorks.intro: This vector database cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does a vector database cost?
faq.answer: Costs depend on data size and queries. Pinecone starts at $0 (free tier) to $70+/month for production. Self-hosted options like Qdrant or Chroma can be cheaper but require infrastructure management.

faq.question: What is RAG?
faq.answer: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines a vector database of your documents with an LLM. When a user asks a question, relevant documents are retrieved and added to the LLM prompt for accurate, grounded answers.

faq.question: Is the Vector Database Cost Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What number of documents should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate number of documents value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Vector Database Cost Calculator?
sections.body: The Vector Database Cost Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your number of documents, avg chunk size, embedding dimensions, you get instant results including total chunks, storage size, one-time embedding cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total chunks right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your number of documents and need to find the right total chunks. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Number of Documents
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your number of documents — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: avg chunk size, embedding dimensions, queries per day, days per month. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total chunks, storage size, one-time embedding cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your number of documents before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total chunks and storage size — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your days per month, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
